text: Competitive trading esports and the convergence of trading cards, prediction markets, and perpetual futures create new user retention mechanisms in crypto. Trading esports tournaments with real money and leaderboards are gaining traction, with platforms like Hyperliquid capturing significant market share. The tokenized trading card market and prediction markets also grow rapidly, offering ownership, opinion monetization, and competition as core engagement drivers. This new '3-Headed Hydra' is reshaping crypto user acquisition and retention in 2026.

text: Hyperliquid ecosystem shows strong growth and market activity with $HYPE token gaining traction. HIP-3 surpassed $200 billion in trading volume, and Hyperliquid's 2025 revenue exceeded $900 million, surpassing the GDP of 10 countries. Hyperliquid also leads in daily revenue generation compared to major blockchains and is noted for its clean revenue model with high fee capture and no incentives. The community is active with meetups in Hong Kong, Turkey, and Dubai, and new perpetual futures and spot trading assets are being listed. The $HYPE token shows structural volatility characteristics with a premium in options markets, and Paradigm is unlocking over 2 million HYPE tokens soon.

text: Highlight Clip Vitalik Buterin: Ethereum is a Spreadsheet with Macros On March 12, 2021, Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin stated in a Tim Ferriss interview that Ethereum is fundamentally a "general-purpose blockchain." He explained that while Bitcoin is a static spreadsheet where users only control specific cells, Ethereum is a "spreadsheet with macros" where accounts can execute code and interact autonomously based on pre-programmed rules. He believes the core of building on Ethereum lies in its autonomy; once an application is deployed, it no longer depends on its creator or any individual to maintain it, guaranteeing it runs strictly by its rules without interference. — link

text: From Charge Plates to Stablecoin Credit > The post explains how payment cards evolved from early merchant credit systems to global networks like Visa and Mastercard > It argues the real disruption in cards is not at the the interface but at the back-end where stablecoins and onchain systems could make improvements. > Stablecoins will only matter in cards if they improve something fundamental in the back end: settlement timing, treasury mobility, prefunding, reconciliation, collateral use, receivables finance, or cost of capital. > If they do not, then they remain an interesting wrapper around an old system Source:

We expect the Fed to hold rates steady until the fourth quarter, while the ECB and BOE keep the option to hike open." — Estelle Ou, economist. Mon: * Musk vs. Altman Trial starts over OpenAI for-profit conversion (it's a legal case about whether OpenAI defrauded Musk). Over the next few weeks, high-profile AI executives, such as Microsoft's CEO Satya Nadella and CTO Kevin Scott, will likely testify. Former OpenAI executives, such as cofounder Ilya Sutskever and Mira Murati, former CTO, may be called. So far, they appear to be anchored." — Anna Wong (BE); infinityhedge: The Fed policy meeting may well be the last for Powell as head of the US central bank. * BoC rate decision * Japan: start of Golden Week, a cluster of national public holidays * Earnings: MSFT, AMZN, META, GOOGL, QCOM Thu: * BOE rate decision * U.S. Q1 GDP * U.S. PCE * Crypto: MegaETH TGE * Earnings: AAPL, SNDK, WDC, RDDT, MA, LLY Fri: * U.S. ISM manufacturing * China Gold Reserves Update * Earnings: CVX, XOM Berkshire Hathaway annual shareholders meeting on Saturday TBA/Whole Week: The focus will be on the fallout of the Middle East conflict, Earnings, Capex, Oil and ofc AI ICYMI: DAY 59 OF WAR: OIL FORECASTS: * Goldman Sachs raised US oil forecast to ~$83/barrel in Q4 (from $75), Brent to ~$90 (from $80). Assumes Hormuz normalizes by end-June. Estimates 14.5mbpd of Middle East production losses are drawing global inventories at a record 11-12mbpd pace in April. Market expected to swing from a 1.8mbpd 2025 surplus to a 9.6mbpd Q2 2026 deficit. "Even sharper demand losses could be required if the supply shock persists longer.": infinityhedge * Morgan Stanley sees Brent averaging $110 this quarter, $100 in Q3, $90 in Q4. * Citi also raised to $110 this quarter, $95 in Q3, $80 in Q4. IRAN–U.S. TALKS FAILED: * Reporters leave Islamabad. Witkoff & Kushner's trip cancelled. Secret Service also departed. Pakistan reopening Islamabad after two weeks of closure. Iran FM said yet to see if the U.S. is truly serious about diplomacy and Netanyahu instructed IDF to strike Hezbollah targets in Lebanon with full force. STRAIT OF HORMUZ: * still effectively closed. * GS estimates 14.5 million barrels per day of ‌Middle ⁠East crude production losses are driving global oil inventories to draw at a record 11-12 million bpd pace in April. * Total crude lost: near 600M barrels (~$60B). text: EU Adopts 20th Russia Sanctions Package, Expands Crypto Ban The EU has approved its 20th sanctions package against Russia, one of the largest in two years, sweeping restrictions on the crypto sector for the first time. Measures include a full ban on transactions with Russian crypto service providers and platforms, as well as prohibitions on the use and support of the digital rouble and the RUBx stablecoin. The package also targets Russia's financial system, imposing transaction bans on 20 Russian banks and additional entities. — link

text: QCP Market Colour, 27 Apr 2026 Another Shot at Peace Geopolitics remains fluid. US–Iran talks stalled again, while a shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner briefly jolted markets, with Trump reportedly among potential targets. Crypto reacted fast. BTC and ETH broke above 79k and 2,400 at Asia open before gains were pared on headlines of Iran–Russia talks, reintroducing escalation risk. Still, the broader trend holds firm: • BTC +14% MTD, 4th straight green week • $2.1B ETF inflows over 9 consecutive sessions • Strategy added $3.8B BTC in April Positioning remains supportive. Funding stays negative, leaving room for a squeeze, while options flows show renewed appetite for upside (90k calls in focus). 82k is the level to watch. A clean break could define the next leg. If risk sentiment holds, BTC may yet have another path higher. Read more:

text: Project Darkbloom: Unlocking Idle Compute for AI Project Darkbloom is an Eigen Labs research initiative that turns idle Apple Silicon Macs into a distributed AI inference network, using a coordinator to route requests to verified Mac nodes, with hardware-backed attestation via Secure Enclave to provide verifiable privacy guarantees Tapping already-purchased hardware where marginal cost is mostly electricity, Darkbloom can price some models at ~half of major aggregators while letting providers keep 95% of revenue - though the coordinator is still a trusted layer, and the harder work lies in code signing, attestation, and handling real-world operating conditions

text: Traditional cross-border remittance giant Western Union's CEO and President Devin McGranahan said its Solana-based, U.S. dollar-backed stablecoin USDPT is in the final stages of preparation and expected to launch next month. USDPT will serve as an alternative to SWIFT for settlements with agents, initially in select countries with key partners. Western Union will also launch the Digital Asset Network to connect crypto wallets with its retail and agent network, and plans to roll out a USD Stable Card later this year across dozens of markets. — link

Spot SOL ETFs recorded net inflows of $9.44 million, while spot XRP ETFs saw net inflows of $15.74 million. — link
